steps.title: Apply the area formula for a square
steps.content: For a square with side length $s$, the area is $A = s^2$. We need to find the area when $s = 65$ cm.

steps.title: Calculate 65 squared
steps.content:
Expand using the perfect square pattern $(60 + 5)^2$:
$$65^2 = 60^2 + 2(60)(5) + 5^2 = 3600 + 600 + 25 = 4225 \text{ cm}^2$$
C is correct.
